meta.title: From ecosystem signal to portfolio activation
meta.scope: Unofficial day-one operating case for Startup Partnerships — France & Southern Europe
meta.disclaimer: A role-specific work sample, not an Anthropic plan or a claim of existing relationships. Public facts are sourced. Support levels are qualitative judgments for programme design — not calibrated probabilities.

worldviews.name: Portfolio activation
worldviews.summary: Optimise for repeatable technical adoption across a partner portfolio.
worldviews.rankingNote: The cohort wins because it creates founder value, a repeatable partner motion and measurable activation — not because it creates the largest audience.

worldviews.name: Product learning
worldviews.summary: Optimise for dense feedback from advanced technical founders.
worldviews.rankingNote: A smaller salon wins when the scarce outcome is product insight rather than activation volume.

worldviews.name: Visibility
worldviews.summary: Optimise for reach and ecosystem presence.
worldviews.rankingNote: Sponsorship wins only under a visibility-first policy; the same factual graph produces a different action because the objective function changed.

nodes.name: Startup Partnerships operator
nodes.summary: Illustrative operating surface derived from the public Anthropic role description.

sources.title: Anthropic — Startup Partnerships, France & Southern Europe

nodes.name: Motier Ventures
nodes.summary: Moulin family investment vehicle: 100 portfolio companies, nearly 40% AI, and La Maison for AI entrepreneurs.

nodes.statement: Create a repeatable Motier portfolio programme that produces five sustained Claude activations by 31 December 2026.

sources.title: Role mandate: portfolio engagement, developer enablement and ROI

nodes.statement: Motier has at least fifteen portfolio companies relevant to a technical Claude programme.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will public research plus partner validation identify at least 15 technically relevant Motier portfolio companies by 15 September 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: A sourced list of at least 15 current portfolio companies with an active AI product, AI-enabled workflow or credible model-evaluation need.

sources.title: 100 portfolio companies; nearly 40% of investments in AI

evidenceFor: 100 portfolio companies; nearly 40% of investments in AI
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: Motier will designate an accountable programme owner.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will Motier designate one programme owner with portfolio access by 30 September 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: A named counterpart accepts responsibility for cohort recruitment, scheduling and follow-up.

sources.title: Illustrative partner-side market — requires direct validation

evidenceFor: Illustrative partner-side market — requires direct validation
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: At least half of recruited teams share an evaluation-to-production need.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will discovery reveal one shared technical need across at least 50% of recruited Motier teams by 15 October 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: Discovery notes from at least 10 teams show a common blocker across 5 or more teams.
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: Security, privacy and procurement constraints are resolvable for a majority of qualified teams.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will at least 60% of qualified teams report no fatal security, privacy or procurement blocker by 31 October 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: Six of ten qualified teams can proceed to a technical evaluation under their stated constraints.
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: The operator can recruit ten qualified technical champions from the portfolio.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will ten qualified technical champions accept the programme invitation by 31 October 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: Ten named technical owners complete intake with a use case and evaluation commitment.
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: Eight teams will complete a structured Claude evaluation.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will eight recruited teams complete an evaluation dataset, baseline and go/no-go decision within 60 days?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: Eight evaluation records contain representative cases, a baseline, a rubric and a recorded decision.
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: Five evaluated teams will sustain qualified usage for 30 days.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will five evaluated teams maintain the agreed qualified-usage threshold for 30 days by 31 December 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: Five distinct portfolio companies cross the agreed activation threshold and remain active for 30 consecutive days.

sources.title: Role mandate: activation and account penetration

evidenceFor: Role mandate: activation and account penetration
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: A cohort evaluation workshop will raise completion versus credits-only access.
nodes.marketQuestion: Will the structured cohort improve completed evaluations by at least 20 percentage points versus a credits-only comparison by 31 December 2026?
nodes.resolutionCriteria: Difference in completion rate is at least 20 percentage points with comparable eligibility criteria.
nodes.decisionAffected: Whether to invest operator time in a Motier portfolio cohort vs another partner.

nodes.statement: No accountable partner owner: recruitment fragments and the partnership remains ceremonial.
Portfolio needs are too heterogeneous for one cohort to create technical value.
Credits generate trials, but no evaluation discipline or sustained production usage.
Earn technical trust before asking for expansion; preserve an honest no-fit outcome.
decisionImplications: Reject vanity programmes without a technical decision

sources.title: Public role: trusted technical partner and rigorous analysis

nodes.statement: Prefer repeatable portfolio activation over one-off ecosystem visibility.
decisionImplications: Prioritise partners with operational portfolio access
Measure activated accounts and programme ROI

sources.title: Public role: portfolio engagement, activation and ROI

nodes.statement: Create direct operating value for AI founders and their communities.
nodes.quote: La Maison, its new venue for AI entrepreneurs and their communities
decisionImplications: Design for a reusable portfolio programme

sources.title: Motier Holding — La Maison and AI portfolio

nodes.statement: Compile organisation, team and target principles into one contextual action rule.
given: Technical trust is a hard constraint
Portfolio activation is the current team objective
Motier has a dense AI portfolio and a founder-community venue
when: One shared need covers at least half the cohort
nodes.because: It creates direct founder value, measurable activation and reusable product feedback.
unless: A fatal product or security mismatch emerges

nodes.name: Portfolio evaluation cohort
nodes.summary: A Build with Claude programme that moves teams from use-case selection to evidence-backed go/no-go decisions.
nodes.expectedEffect: Increase evaluation completion and sustained activation while generating structured product feedback.

sources.title: Role mandate: technical enablement and portfolio programmes

nodes.name: Technical founder salon
nodes.summary: A small-room session optimised for high-density product feedback.
nodes.expectedEffect: Higher learning density, lower activation volume.

sources.title: Role mandate: founder salons and product feedback

nodes.name: Flagship event sponsorship
nodes.summary: A visibility-led ecosystem play with weak direct causality to activation unless paired with enablement.
nodes.expectedEffect: Reach and awareness, with uncertain downstream adoption.

nodes.statement: Run discovery, secure a partner owner, then launch a Motier portfolio evaluation cohort.
nodes.rationale: Best fit under portfolio activation: it connects stakeholder mapping, developer enablement, product feedback and measurable ROI.

nodes.statement: Run a private technical founder salon before a scaled programme.
nodes.rationale: Wins when the team optimises for product learning rather than activation volume.

nodes.statement: Lead with flagship event sponsorship.
nodes.rationale: Wins only under a visibility-first policy and has the weakest causal path to sustained adoption.

nodes.name: Build with Claude — Evaluation Kit
nodes.summary: Founder briefing, representative-case template, baseline rubric, cost worksheet and 30-day follow-up protocol.

nodes.statement: Portfolio programme scorecard

sources.title: Role mandate: own analytics and programme ROI

nodes.statement: Motier reports 100 portfolio companies and nearly 40% of investments focused on AI.

nodes.warrant: A dense AI portfolio raises the chance of recruiting qualified teams, but portfolio size alone does not prove a shared need or an accountable programme owner.
nodes.claim: Raise the terminal activation market from weakly supported to uncertain; keep shared-need and owner rebuttals active.

sources.title: Public evidence behind the belief update

nodes.note: Target-specific state after public portfolio-density evidence.

sources.title: Illustrative update grounded in Motier public data

nodes.note: Strong public proxy; still requires company-level qualification.

edges.mechanism: Objective resolves through the terminal account market.
A denser qualified portfolio increases the reachable champion pool.
A partner owner turns portfolio access into coordinated recruitment.
Shared need makes cohort content specific enough to complete.
Resolvable constraints preserve the path from evaluation to production usage.
Completed evaluations require qualified technical owners.
Sustained usage requires a completed technical evaluation.
Structured cases, baselines and follow-up should improve completion versus credits alone.
Absence of an owner blocks coordinated delivery.
Heterogeneity invalidates a single cohort design.
Trials without discipline may not become production usage.
